items.title: India news: Ambedkar and the idea of Dalit upliftment

items.title: Germany: 1 in 4 residents has 'immigration history'
items.description: Every fourth person living in Germany has either moved there from abroad or been born to parents who are both immigrants. The new statistics are a modest increase on the 2024 figures.

items.title: Ukulele club's 600 members may be strumming their way to better brain health

items.title: How the AFL's gamble with conflict of interest concerns didn't pay off

items.title: US maritime restrictions increase economic pressure on Iran

items.title: Donald Trump 'hasn't read the psychology of Iranians well at all', military expert says
items.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, former British Military Intelligence officer Philip Ingram says that "Donald Trump hasn't read the psychology of the Iranians well at all" adding that "the Iranians are not going to give in, and therefore Donald Trump either has to continue the blockade and deal with the impact that that's going to have on global economies [...] or go back to a bombing campaign".

Democrat Eric Swalwell resigned after misconduct allegations, while Republican Tony Gonzales said he would retire following pressure over an affair with a former aide, deepening turmoil in the House of Representatives.

items.title: EU doubles steel tariffs to 50% to curb surge of cheap Chinese imports
items.description: EU lawmakers and member states agreed on Monday to double tariffs on foreign steel to 50%, aiming to shield the bloc’s struggling industry from a surge in cheap Chinese imports. The deal also cuts duty-free import volumes by 47%. Trade chief Maros Sefcovic warned global overcapacity threatens Europe’s industrial strength.

items.description: Geordin Hill-Lewis has secured the top job in Cape Town, but his ambitions are already stretching well beyond the city, as he positions himself for national leadership.

items.title: Middle East war live: Israel-Lebanon talks set as Hezbollah urges Beirut to cancel negotiations
items.description: The leader of the Lebanese militant Hezbollah group urged Lebanon on Monday to pull out of direct talks with Israel set to take place in Washington the following day, the first such talks in decades. Naim Kassem spoke in a televised address on the eve of the scheduled meeting between Lebanon and Israel's ambassadors to the United States, as both sides set a framework for negotiations. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.title: Hungary will be "a tough partner, but a partner" on Ukraine, expert says
items.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Lukáš Macek, Director of the Greater Europe project at the Jacques Delors Centre, says on the question of Ukraine the new Hungarian government led by Peter Magyar is likely to stop obstructing aid for Ukraine and will be "a tough partner, but a partner nonetheless" on the issue of Ukrainian accession to the EU.

items.title: Milei pleads for patience as growth slows and approval falls
items.description: “We know that the last months were hard. That’s why we’re asking for patience. This is the right path," says President Milei in social media post. Leer más

items.title: Dieselgate: Audi bosses, engineers set for long trial as plea deal fails
items.description: Two former Audi bosses and two engineers are facing charges over "Dieselgate," a massive emissions scandal that still haunts Germany's automobile sector. As no plea deals could be reached, a long trial seems likely.

items.title: Germany news: Merz says fuel tax lowered to ease price surge
items.description: Berlin is cutting fuel tax and weapons maker Rheinmetall prepares to build missiles in a new venture. A fisherman triggered a massive search effort before being found drunk and asleep in a car.

items.title: Iran war: Talks collapse is bad news for ordinary Iranians
items.description: The collapse of the US-Iran talks has cast fresh doubt over whether the two sides can bridge the gap dividing them. But whether diplomacy will succeed also depends on a range of challenges inside Iran.

items.title: UK seeks jail terms for tech bosses over non-consensual sex images
items.description: The Labour government, which holds a working majority in the House of Commons, has tabled an amendment to the Crime and Policing bill currently before parliament

items.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
items.description: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

items.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
items.description: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

items.title: World Bank sees Argentina’s economy growing 3.6% this year
items.description: Economy will grow 3.6% this year and 3.7% next year; Multilateral institution’s forecast means Argentina is currently on track to record three years of consecutive growth for first time since 2008. Leer más

items.title: Stories that caught our eye: April 2 to 10
items.description: A selection of the stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Pope Leo brushes off Trump criticism amid growing Vatican–U.S. tensions over Iran war
items.description: Pope Leo XIV says he will not be deterred by criticism from President Trump, vowing to continue his calls for peace as tensions escalate between the Vatican and Washington over the Iran conflict.

items.title: Hezbollah leader urges Lebanon’s government to pull out of Israel talks
items.description: Naim Qassem says planned talks in Washington, DC, are a ploy to pressure Hezbollah into laying down its weapons.

items.title: Haiti: at least 30 dead in stampede at UNESCO‑listed Citadelle Laferrière fortress
items.description: At least 30 people died on Saturday, 11 April 2026, after a stampede at the Citadelle Laferrière near Milot, a major tourist site in the north of Haiti.

items.title: US-Vatican feud over Iran: Pope Leo pushes back on Trump's criticism
items.description: The Pope pushed back Monday on President Donald Trump’s criticism of him over the US-Israel war in Iran, telling reporters that the Vatican’s appeals for peace and reconciliation are rooted in the Gospel, and that he doesn’t fear the Trump administration. Leo XIV's comments came as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz appeared to have come to a halt, an intelligence firm said, and oil prices resumed their climb after Trump announced on social media that the United States would blockade the waterway.

items.title: Germany: Police suspect antisemitism in Israeli restaurant attack in Munich
items.description: Police and restaurant owners believe antisemitism motivated the Munich attack. Nobody was hurt, but three windows were broken, causing damage worth several thousand euros. This comes amid tension over the war in Iran.

items.title: Report: Milei government not supporting campaign to restore dictatorship identities
items.description: Milei administration has interrupted sending of DNA kits to consulates nationwide for those who have doubts about their identity and suspect they may be children of disappeared. Leer más

items.title: Crans-Montana mayor says unaware of six-year safety lapse before Swiss bar fire that killed 41
items.description: Crans-Montana Mayor Nicolas Feraud said on Monday he was unaware safety checks had not been conducted for six years at a Swiss ski resort bar where a New Year fire killed 41 people. Prosecutors are investigating the blaze, with nine people, including Feraud, under criminal investigation over the disaster.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV pushes back as Donald Trump criticism escalates
items.description: US-born Pope Leo XIV pushed back Monday on President Donald Trump’s broadside against him over the U.S.-Israel war in Iran, telling reporters that the Vatican’s appeals for peace and reconciliation are rooted in the Gospel, and that he doesn’t fear the Trump administration.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV urges immediate ceasefire as Israel-Hezbollah clashes rage
items.description: Pope Leo XIV called for an immediate ceasefire on Sunday as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues, urging all parties to respect international law and protect civilians.

items.title: The iceman goeth
items.description: Resources are the name of the game in today’s world and the amendments to the 2010 Glaciers Law should be seen within that context. Leer más

items.title: Iran's new Supreme Leader is reportedly recovering from severe wounds
items.description: The news agency Reuters said on Sunday that Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei was recovering from severe and disfiguring wounds from the airstrike that killed his father, Ali Khamenei. Iran's leader has not been seen in public since his appointment.

items.title: At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
items.description: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Orban ousted: What Magyar’s victory means for Hungary and the EU
items.description: Peter Magyar's landslide victory over Viktor Orban in Sunday’s Hungarian legislative elections marks a seismic shift for Hungary and the EU, but Brussels may need to temper its enthusiasm. On the rule of law, migration and LGBTQ rights, the road ahead is neither straight nor guaranteed.

items.title: Hungary: Peter Magyar's election heralds thaw in EU ties
items.description: Following the ouster of Russia-friendly Viktor Orban after 16 years, Europe is now waiting to see if his designated successor Peter Magyar can make good on his promises to repair ties with Brussels.

items.title: Nigeria Air Force orders investigation after dozens killed in airstrike
items.description: There are reports that more than 100 people were killed, although the authorities have not commented.

items.title: Trump deletes Jesus post of himself after outcry
items.description: Asked about the post, Mr. Trump denied that he was trying to look like Jesus Christ

items.title: News24 | Republicans block bid to rein in Trump’s war powers, Democrats vow to force a vote
items.description: US House of Representatives Republicans blocked a Democratic effort to end US attacks on Iran, as Donald Trump’s party continued to prevent efforts to rein in the Republican president’s war powers.

items.title: Environmental NGOs seek ‘largest collective lawsuit in history’ in defence of Argentina’s glaciers
items.description: Environmental groups call on members of the public to join a massive collective lawsuit against President Javier Milei’s changes to Argentina’s Glacier Law, which were approved by the lower house in the early hours of Thursday morning. Leer más

items.title: Ireland clears refinery blockade as fuel protests trigger nationwide shortages
items.description: Authorities used force to reopen the country's only refinery, after days of disruption over petrol prices left fuel pumps dry and threatened emergency services operations.

items.title: Hungary election: Orban concedes 'painful' defeat to Magyar
items.description: Veteran Prime Minister Viktor Orban has acknowledged the landslide victory of Peter Magyar and his Tisza party in Hungary's parliamentary election. Magyar has promised sweeping reforms.

items.title: Ghanaian Premier League player Dominic Frimpong killed during attack on team bus
items.description: Football fans in Ghana are in mourning after the death of Berekum Chelsea player Dominic Frimpong. The 20-year-old winger was killed in a suspected armed robbery on the team's bus, the country's football association said on Monday.

items.title: Trump lambasts Pope Leo XIV, extending feud over Iran war with first American pontiff
items.description: Pope Leo, who is known for choosing his words carefully, ‌has emerged as an outspoken ​critic of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran that ⁠began on February 28

items.title: Libya to have first unified state budget in 13 years
items.description: Libya's rival eastern and western legislative bodies have signed a US-mediated agreement to unify public spending across the divided country for the first time in over a decade, the central bank said Saturday.

items.title: Super Netball expansion delayed as TV talks drag on
items.description: Two new teams were expected to join the league in 2027 to make Super Netball a 10-team competition, but slow broadcast negotiations are holding up the process.

items.title: 'You did it dad': The ordinary people who took their case to the highest court
items.description: From love and freedom to native title and the right to vote, these are some of the High Court cases that have helped define our nation, and the ordinary people behind them.

items.title: InterCement deal ‘once in a lifetime opportunity,’ Mindlin says
items.description: Argentine energy mogul Marcelo Mindlin is leading a group of investors and creditors seeking to restructure Brazil’s InterCement, the parent company of Loma Negra SA. Leer más

items.title: After New York setback, mystery surrounding Burford’s hidden YPF litigation partners grows
items.description: Over and above the forfeited gains of the law office that litigated against Argentina, the big losers of the recent New York Court of Appeals ruling over the nationalisation of YPF are the investors who bought up part of the suit. The story of a trial which still shrouds identities – a mystery only a US judge will be able to elucidate. Leer más

items.title: The Dutch village at risk of being demolished
items.description: Moerdijk has been earmarked for removal, to make way for a vast electricity substation.

items.title: Pope says he will continue to speak out against war after Trump attack
items.description: “I don’t ‌want to get into a debate with him,” Pope Leo said; “I don’t think that ‌the message of the ​Gospel is meant to be abused in the way ⁠that some people are doing,” he said

items.title: News24 | ANALYSIS | Africa and the Middle East crisis: better prepared, but not insulated
items.description: The timing couldn’t be worse for a continent just beginning to recover from two recent global shocks, write Ronak Gopaldas and Menzi Ndhlovu in ISS Today.

items.title: Did the ayatollahs get one over on Trump?
items.description: Even though Trump is desperately looking for an excuse to call it quits, he could change his mind yet again when it dawns on him that the remnants of the Iranian regime are making him look like a loser. Leer más

items.title: Boosting women's role in politics, Dutch-style
items.description: Some 504 women were elected in recent Dutch local elections, with a "Vote for a Woman" campaign boosting their chances to gather preferential votes. Despite the record figure, women remain underrepresented.

items.title: Israeli fire kills three in Gaza following new ceasefire talks
items.description: Medics said the strike had hit a group of men outside a school in Deir al-Balah in the central Gaza Strip. There was no immediate comment by the Israeli military

items.title: Israel vows to fight on as Trump grants Iran ceasefire
items.description: United States and Iran agree a ceasefire and are due to launch talks in Pakistan aimed at reaching a longer-term deal, but crucial sticking points remain. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Trump ‘not a big fan’ of Pope Leo XIV who made plea for peace
items.description: US President Donald Trump told reporters that he is “not a big fan” of Pope Leo XIV, after the global leader of Catholics made a plea for peace.

items.title: Government greets Hungary’s new leader after Milei ally Orbán gets boot
items.description: Milei’s Foreign Minister Pablo Quirno extends greeting to Hungary’s newly elected Prime Minister Péter Magyar after European nation votes to oust Viktor Orbán and end populist’s 16 years in power. Leer más

items.title: Poland withdraws from treaty banning antipersonnel mines, will use them to defend against Russia
items.description: Poland, which ratified the document in 2012 and completed the destruction of its domestic anti-personnel mine stockpile in 2016, withdrew from the treaty on February 20, 2026 and says it plans to renew manufacturing weapons

items.title: Peru presidential election results delayed after thousands get one-day voting extension
items.description: The problems prompted electoral authorities to allow 63,300 residents of Peru’s capital, Lima, to vote on April 13

items.title: Benin's opposition candidate concedes defeat to Wadagni in presidential vote
items.description: Benin opposition candidate Paul Hounkpe suffered a bruising election loss on Monday after conceding to favourite Romuald Wadagni in Benin's presidential vote.

items.title: Argentina looks to global debt markets to finance energy boom
items.description: Corporate borrowers are turning to global debt markets to fund an energy-driven expansion. Leer más

items.title: Trump posts and then deletes Jesus-like AI image of himself
items.description: The US president denied looking like Jesus in the image, saying he thought it was himself "as a doctor." Trump did not explain why he deleted the post.

items.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
items.description: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the communist dictatorship.

items.title: News24 | Britney Spears enters rehab facility following arrest amid ongoing personal struggles
items.description: US pop superstar Britney Spears has checked herself into rehab following her arrest last month on suspicion of driving while intoxicated near Los Angeles.

items.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive
items.description: NASA's renewed push to get a human mission to the moon and Mars is neither cheap, nor for the faint of heart. But who ends up covering the costs and what does everyone still on Earth get out of all this?

items.title: Mauritius vows to pursue Chagos sovereignty after UK deal collapses
items.description: Mauritius has pledged to “spare no effort” to reclaim control of the Chagos Islands after a proposed handover deal by Keir Starmer collapsed amid shifting U.S. support.

items.title: Peru frontrunner Keiko Fujimori vows to expel migrants, further Latin America's rightward tilt
items.description: Keiko Fujimori, 50-year-old daughter of late ex-president Alberto Fujimori, is one of 35 candidates running to be Peru's next leader in Sunday's election. Leer más

items.title: Hungary for change: On Victor Orban’s ouster
items.description: Peter Magyar’s win may reverse hard-right domestic and foreign policies

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Benin’s Wadagni wins presidential election with landslide 94% of votes
items.description: The incoming president faces serious security issues in the north of the country and challenges to living standards.

items.title: Why Gout's incredible run deserves equal measures of praise and patience
items.description: Gout Gout was on the lips of every athletics fan across the globe on the weekend, but elements of his record-breaking run need to be treated with caution.

items.title: Hungary's Orban defeated: France's far-right loses an ally
items.description: Congratulations from across Europe poured in Monday for Hungarian political newcomer Peter Magyar, who defeated nationalist Viktor Orban in elections seen as a blow to right-wing populism worldwide. In France, the outcome represents a setback for Marine Le Pen’s far-right party, as FRANCE 24’s French Politics Editor Marc Perelman explains.

items.title: Slovakia's Fico ready to cooperate with Hungary's new Prime Minister
items.description: Under Fico Slovakia has been ‌a close ally of Hungary, with both ​neighbours maintaining warm ties with Moscow, opposing European Union sanctions and keeping up purchases of ⁠Russian oil and gas

items.title: Airstrike in Nigeria kills dozens, civilians feared among victims
items.description: At least 56 people were killed and 14 injured after a military airstrike hit a market in northeastern Nigeria, according to a UN report, with locals and rights groups warning that civilians were among those killed.

items.title: French court jails Lafarge former CEO for funding IS in Syria
items.description: A French court on Monday fined the cement group Lafarge over $1.3 million and sentenced its former boss to six years in prison for paying protection money to the Islamic State group and other jihadists to maintain its business in war-torn Syria. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

items.title: News24 | US to begin blockade of Iranian ports Monday, military says
items.description: The US military said on Sunday it would begin a blockade of all Iranian ports within hours, after peace talks in Pakistan collapsed with Washington blaming the Islamic republic’s refusal to abandon its nuclear ambitions.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV begins historic Algeria visit on first leg of Africa tour
items.description: Pope Leo XIV has begun a historic visit to Algeria, marking the first-ever papal trip to the country and launching an 11-day African tour focused on interfaith dialogue and regional engagement.

items.title: A U.S. military blockade of Iran's ports takes effect. What does that mean?
items.description: A U.S. blockade of Iranian ports has gone into effect Monday, while President Trump claims Iran wants to make a deal.

items.title: Ukrainian civilians abducted, tortured in Russian prisons
items.description: Russia has been holding thousands of Ukrainian civilians behind bars for years, in breach of international humanitarian law. Families have been fighting for their loved ones to be released, some for more than a decade.

items.title: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
items.description: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

items.title: Interfaith dialogue: 'The messenger of peace in the name of Pope Leo XIV is coming to visit us'
items.description: As Pope Leo XIV makes a historic visit to Algeria, in the spirit of interfaith dialogue, François Picard is pleased to welcome Father Fred Wekesa, Rector of the Basilica of Saint Augustine in Annaba. From his experience of living over a decade in Algeria, he challenges inherited narratives shaped by the violence of the 1990s, highlighting the present reality of coexistence and tolerance. He argues that interreligious dialogue must extend beyond institutions into families and daily life.

items.title: Hollywood stars sign open letter opposing Paramount-Warner Bros deal
Wanted: A governing agenda for Javier Milei
items.description: A perception of dissonance between what the wider world and the public here see of Milei and his government is bad news for the administration. Leer más

items.title: Safety alert issued amid explosion of illegal peptide use
items.description: The medicines regulator issues a health alert about the risks of using unregulated peptides amid a surge in the use of potentially unsafe injectables.

items.title: Russian psyops and the dance of the candidates
items.description: The Milei administration is on the back foot, tired, hanging off the ropes, but is nowhere near knockout territory. Leer más

items.title: France: Parliament debates bill to simplify return of stolen artworks
items.description: It still has tens of thousands of artworks and artefacts that it looted from its colonial empire. Now, France is debating a bill to simplify returning them to their countries of origin.

items.title: Germany marks 69th birthday of zoo’s oldest gorilla
items.description: Berlin Zoo celebrates Fatou, a 69‑year‑old western lowland gorilla, highlighting how specialised care in captivity can extend the lives of endangered species.

items.title: News24 | Orban concedes defeat to Magyar after 16-year rule in Hungary
items.description: Nationalist Viktor Orban, who has ruled Hungary for 16 years, on Sunday conceded defeat to conservative Peter Magyar in parliamentary elections.

items.title: Benin heads to a pivotal transition as votes are counted
items.description: Benin began counting ballots Sunday after a presidential election that could extend a decade of economic growth while testing the country’s democratic balance. Finance Minister Romuald Wadagni is the frontrunner to succeed outgoing president Patrice Talon, who is stepping down after two terms.

items.title: US stocks finish higher amid hopes for US-Iran deal as oil price gains moderate
items.description: Markets are betting on a possible US-Iran deal after US President Donald Trump said Iran would “like to make a deal, very badly”.

items.title: End of the Orban era: Hungary and Europe breathe again
items.description: Viktor Orban's Fidesz party was swept aside in Hungary's parliamentary election. His defeat is a blow for Putin, Trump and populists the world over. Election winner Peter Magyar is promising major change.

items.title: Prosecutors probe US$10,000 New York flights linked to Cabinet Chief Adorni
items.description: Investigation is seeking to establish who paid for the return journey of Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni and his wife, Bettina Angeletti, from New York after Argentina Week roadshow. Leer más

items.title: Giant echidna fossil rewrites understanding of Victoria's prehistoric past
items.description: A fossil hidden in a museum collection for 119 years has revealed giant echidnas once roamed Victoria's east, filling a major gap in Australia's prehistoric record.

items.title: Trump says he won't apologize to Pope Leo after criticism
items.description: President Donald Trump refused to apologize to Pope Leo XIV on Monday after criticizing the pontiff for his opposition to the war in Iran — and he sought to explain away a now-deleted social media post depicting himself as Jesus by saying he had thought the image was of him as a doctor. Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has called Trump's remarks "unacceptable". FRANCE 24's Seema Gupta reports from Rome.

items.title: World's oldest gorilla celebrates 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo
items.description: Lady Fatou, known as the "grand dame" of the Berlin Zoo, was certified last year by Guiness as the oldest living gorilla in the world.

items.title: News24 | Man accused of ‘ruthless exploitation’ over pressuring wife to sell sex in Sweden
items.description: A Swedish man suspected of exploiting his “vulnerable” wife to have sex with scores of men for payment, is on trial for aggravated pimping, rape and assault.

items.title: Starc honoured as Wisden slams England's 'feckless' Ashes
items.description: Australia's Ashes ace Mitchell Starc is named the leading men's cricketer of the year by the Wisden Cricketers' Almanack after taking 55 wickets in 2025.

items.title: Oil prices surge after U.S.-Iran peace talks break down
items.description: The U.S. said on Sunday that it will soon block ships from Iranian ports. It comes after the U.S. and Iran failed to reach an agreement during face-to-face peace talks, sending oil prices surging.

items.title: On patrol with Canadian forces securing the Arctic as global threats grow
items.description: Across 5,200 km of Canada's Arctic, its military spent two months on a mission to meet the challenges of a changing north.

items.title: MoS Margherita attends mourning ceremony for late Iranian leader Ayatollah Khamenei
items.description: The ceremony was organised by the Iranian embassy on Sunday (April 12, 2026) to pay respect to the departed leader on the 40th day (Chehelum) of his demise

items.title: Colombia to cull up to 80 hippos linked to drug lord Pablo Escobar
items.description: Introduced by Pablo Escobar in the 1980s, the hippos are a tourist attraction but a headache for authorities and locals.

items.title: Iranian crude returns to India after seven years as tankers dock at key ports
items.description: The one-month exemption permitted the sale of Iranian oil already in transit, aimed at easing global supply disruptions and containing prices

items.title: In Ethiopia, fuel prices overshadow this year's Easter celebrations
items.description: As millions of Ethiopians marked Good Friday in the capital, Addis Ababa on Friday, many were continuing to suffer the impacts of the Middle East crisis, which has sparked chronic fuel shortages and rising costs of basic commodities.

items.title: UFOs: 'Most are reported by people out for an evening smoke'
items.description: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.

items.title: Lebanon: Red Cross teams inspect wreckage after Israeli airstrikes damage ambulances
items.description: Lebanon saw further damage on Monday, 13 April 2026, as Lebanese Red Cross volunteers inspected ambulances hit by Israeli strikes in the coastal city of Tyre, while rescue teams cleared debris in the nearby village of Maaroub.

items.title: Hungarians look to changed future after pro-EU Magyar's election landslide
items.description: Hungarians were waking up to a ​political earthquake on Monday, after a landslide victory for the centre-right opposition reverberated everywhere from Washington to Kyiv, sending local markets surging and turning Budapest into a party zone. Magyar has pledged a sweeping anti-corruption drive, including stronger judicial independence and tougher public procurement rules to help secure the release of the EU funds.

items.title: Luxury brands face profits squeeze as Iran conflict shrinks Dubai Mall sales
items.description: Luxury brands in March reported sales drops of 30-50% at ​the Mall of the Emirates

items.title: Adorni under renewed scrutiny over another privately arranged mortgage
items.description: Another apartment financed with dollars from private lenders: Cabinet chief was given a US$100,000-loan from two women on November 15, 2024, when he was serving as Milei’s presidential spokesperson. Leer más

items.title: Trump's threat raises risks and leaves predicaments unchanged
items.description: The conflict is now a test of wills - Iran’s capacity to absorb strikes versus Trump’s tolerance for the war's costs.

items.title: French lawmakers to debate bill to return looted colonial-era art
items.description: France's lawmakers will on Monday debate a bill to simplify the return of artworks looted during the colonial era to their countries of origin. France still has in its possession tens of thousands of artworks and other prized artefacts that it looted from its colonial empire. FRANCE 24's Carys Garland reports.

items.title: German Bundeswehr says military-aged men can travel freely
items.description: A new rule requiring men to get permission to travel abroad recently put Defense Minister Boris Pistorius under pressure. He says he has fixed the situation, but the criticism continues.

items.title: News24 | Italy's Meloni issues rare criticism of Trump after his attack on pope
items.description: US President Donald Trump’s attack on Pope Leo was “unacceptable,” Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said on Monday, joining politicians of all colours in springing to the pontiff’s defence.

items.title: Argentina’s Congress approves glacier law in win for Milei and copper miners
items.description: Reform, which miners had lobbied for, gives provincial governors control over deciding whether a glacier or permafrost formation is a key source of water downstream. If the provinces decide they aren’t, miners can exploit them. Leer más

items.title: US-Iran talks in Pakistan end without breakthrough
items.description: Marathon negotiations between the United States and Iran in the Pakistani capital ended early Sunday without a breakthrough, with both sides failing to bridge deep differences over nuclear issues, the Strait of Hormuz, and sanctions relief, officials said.

items.title: From chainsaw to pay rise: Argentina’s ministers cash in despite Milei austerity drive
items.description: Government decree lifts top officials’ wages to over eight million pesos a month, fuelling debate over austerity promises; Salary surge contrasts with Javier Milei’s pledge to cut political privilege. Leer más

items.title: Brazil’s fugitive ex-intel chief detained by US immigration authorities

items.title: Argentina to play Honduras and Iceland in pre-World Cup friendlies
items.description: 'La Scaloneta' will face Honduras on June 6 in Texas and Iceland’s senior side on June 9 in Alabama ahead of World Cup kick-off. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Tankers steering clear of Hormuz ahead of a US blockade, oil prices spike
items.description: Oil tankers are steering clear of the Strait of Hormuz ahead of a US blockade following failed peace talks ‌between the US and Iran over the weekend, shipping data showed.

items.title: Palestinian rights groups file legal action against defence minister over Israel arms exports
items.description: A group of Palestinian human rights organisations launch legal action against the defence minister seeking transparency over Australia's arms exports to Israel.

items.title: Prime minister leads tributes after death of NT's first female senator
items.description: A former Arnhem Land schoolteacher who blazed a trail as the Northern Territory’s first female senator, Trish Crossin, has died at age 70.

items.title: Philippines accuses China of using cyanide to poison South China Sea atoll
items.description: Officials warn cyanide could harm marine life and weaken the reef supporting a grounded warship.

items.title: News24 | Deadline passes for US blockade of Hormuz, Iran threatens retaliation against Gulf states
items.description: The deadline passed on Monday for the start of a US military blockade of ships leaving Iran’s ports, and Tehran threatened to ‌retaliate against ports of its Gulf neighbours after weekend talks on ending the war broke down.

items.title: Eight to be awarded for bravery in Bondi Junction stabbing
items.description: Six people were killed and ten injured after a man went on a stabbing rampage in an Australian mall.

items.title: Lebanon will need '1 billion dollars just to keep humanitarian situation afloat', minister says
items.description: FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with the Lebanese Minister for Social Affairs, Hanin Sayyed, about the humanitarian crisis in Lebanon in the wake of the Israeli attacks and ground invasion. She says that if the war continues and displacement in Lebanon continues at the current pace, the country will need "1 billion dollars in a 12-month period to be able to just keep the humanitarian situation afloat".

items.title: 'It's a special thing to be on Planet Earth': Artemis crew welcomed home in Houston
items.description: The four astronauts flew around the Moon in a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans in history.

items.title: Burkina Faso: Moving forward without democracy
items.description: Burkina Faso's junta leader Ibrahim Traore rejects democracy, while a new alliance in exile pushes for its return — but Traore holds the advantage.

items.title: Argentines plunge into debt for bills, cars and birthday parties

items.title: Argentina tweaks FX rules after cost of getting dollars out surges

items.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
items.description: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

items.title: Cape Town mayor elected to lead S.Africa's second-largest party
items.description: South Africa's second-largest party, the Democratic Alliance, elected Cape Town's mayor Sunday to lead it into the 2029 national polls, stepping up a drive to take votes away from the faltering ANC.

items.title: Benin leans into painful past to encourage cultural tourism
items.description: Benin is leaning into its painful history as a key port in the transatlantic slave trade to encourage tourism.

items.title: Flag, anthem restrictions dropped for Russian, Belarusian swimmers
items.description: The move signals a further easing of restrictions on athletes from the two countries by sports-governing bodies.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Eduardo Coudet makes impressive start at River Plate
items.description: River are in the Copa Sudamericana for the first time since winning it in 2014 in Marcelo Gallardo’s first year in charge, a title which kickstarted one of the greatest coaching tenures in history. Now, his replacement would love to launch a dynasty of his own, with the cup as a launching pad. Leer más

items.title: Explainer: Milei’s attempt to change Argentina’s glacier protection law
items.description: Argentina’s Glacier Law reform push is a battle between mining ambitions and environmental protection; Milei’s proposed changes could reshape glacier safeguards, expand mining activity, and shift power to provincial governments. Leer más

items.title: Iran executed at least 1,639 in 2025, more hangings feared: NGOs
items.description: The number of executions represented an increase of 6% on the 975 people Iran put to death in 2024, and also included 48 women, Norway-based Iran Human Rights (IHR) and Paris-based Together Against the Death Penalty (ECPM) said in their joint annual report

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
items.description: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

items.title: Pressure on Adorni builds as investigators probe assets, travel, bank details
items.description: President Javier Milei’s Cabinet chief is facing mounting questions over his finances after journalistic investigations uncovered inconsistencies, unexplained asset growth and transactions involving third parties; Judge requests banking secrecy rules to be lifted to aid probe. Leer más

items.title: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?
items.description: The US military is gambling on the blockade denying Iran vital trade - but questions remain on whether the bet will pay off.

items.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Iranian ports after peace talks fail
items.description: On Sunday, President Trump said the U.S. would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after negotiations between the U.S. and Iran broke down over the weekend.

items.title: US, Iran hold separate talks with Pakistan's PM ahead of negotiations
items.description: US and Iranian officials held separate talks with Pakistan’s prime minister on Saturday amid a ceasefire made fragile by deep disagreements and unabated fighting in Lebanon. International media reported that the two countries had also started direct talks in the afternoon.

items.title: Turkey, Somalia to start first offshore drilling project at Somalian coast
items.description: A Turkish drilling ship docked at the port of Mogadishu on Friday ahead of Somalia's first offshore oil drilling project, the two countries announced.

items.title: Pro-EU Hungarian PM-elect Magyar vows to bring in 'new era'
items.description: From Madrid to Helsinki, many hope that Hungary's premier-elect Peter Magyar's win will help unshackle the 27-nation European Union as it faces hybrid warfare attacks from Moscow, an antagonistic Washington and Beijing's economic pressure. EU leaders had been increasingly frustrated with Orbán over his takeover of democratic institutions and vetoing of strategic action like a 90-billion-euro loan for Ukraine. It remains to be seen whether those hopes will be fulfilled, as FRANCE 24's Europe Editor Armen Georgian reports.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: 300 convictions: Nigeria sees mass trial for terrorism suspects
items.description: A court in the Nigerian capital of Abuja on Friday convicted more than 300 terrorism suspects in a mass trial that spanned four days.

items.title: What impact will the Iran war have on the wedding industry?
items.description: The wedding industry in the UK is gauging the impact of the war with Iran. Despite riding fuel prices putting pressure on supply chains, vendors say they are prepared to absorb higher costs.

items.title: South Korea, Poland to upgrade ties as Donald Tusk calls Seoul key ally after U.S.
items.description: South ⁠Korean President Lee said the countries would further expand defence ⁠industry cooperation under a $44.2 billion framework pact signed in 2022

items.title: Viktor Orbán's Hungarian experiment runs out of steam
items.description: Péter Magyar's historic win offers peace for a country exhausted by the tensions of Viktor Orbán's rule, Nick Thorpe reports from Budapest.

items.title: Trump deletes image of himself as Jesus-like saviour after backlash
items.description: Trump draws criticism from church leaders and conservatives over AI-Jesus image and pope remarks.

items.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
items.description: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

items.title: News24 | Sentebale launches legal action against Prince Harry over ‘reputational harm’
items.description: Sentebale, a charity co-founded by Prince Harry, is suing him for harming its reputation through a media campaign.

items.title: News24 | ‘An eye for everything’: Queen Elizabeth’s iconic wardrobe on display at UK exhibition
items.description: The exhibition “Queen Elizabeth II: Her Life in Style”, which opened at Buckingham Palace, showcases 300 outfits, including her wedding and coronation gowns.

items.title: 4 policemen injured in militant attack on polio team in Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
items.description: The injured were shifted to a nearby hospital for treatment, officials said

items.title: Anthropic talking to the Trump administration about its next AI model, co-founder says
Global markets slide as Donald Trump orders Strait of Hormuz blockade
items.description: Global markets have been rattled after Donald Trump announced a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions following failed peace talks with Iran.

items.title: 5 things to know about Péter Magyar, Hungary's new prime minister
items.description: Magyar ended Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's 16-year grip on power in a landslide victory on Sunday. The former Orbán loyalist burst onto the scene as an opposition leader in 2024.

items.title: France: Ex-Lafarge CEO jailed for funding Syrian jihadists
items.description: The world's largest cement maker paid off terrorist groups in Syria in order to keep manufacturing there, judges in Paris have ruled.

items.title: Celebrities and fans pay tributes to Asha Bhosle
items.description: Asha Bhosle died in Mumbai on Sunday after being admitted to the hospital following a heart attack.

items.title: Trail mix and water help 87yo woman survive near-freezing night in dense forest
items.description: Police say the woman wandered down a rabbit track after becoming disoriented on a weekend bushwalk.

items.description: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

items.title: Bernie Sanders vows to push resolution to block US weapons to Israel
items.description: Bill is unlikely to pass in Republican-controlled Senate, but it could test waning support for Israel among Democrats.

items.title: Lufthansa pilots launch 2-day strike, cabin crew say they will strike right after
items.description: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. As hundreds of flights were canceled, the UFO cabin crew union said it would also strike on Wednesday and Thursday.

items.title: News24 | Artemis crew gets ready for Earth splashdown after historic Moon mission
items.description: The four Artemis II astronauts, returning from the world’s first crewed Moon voyage in over half a century, hurtled back toward Earth aboard their gumdrop-shaped Orion spacecraft, headed for a splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off Southern California.

items.title: Israel says troops fighting Hezbollah surrounded key Lebanon town
items.description: "The forces of the 98th Division have completed the encirclement of the town of Bint Jbeil and have begun an assault on it," the military's Arabic-language spokesman Colonel Avichay Adraee said on X.

items.description: The U.S. president said his threat to impose 50% tariffs on goods from countries that sold weapons to Iran was aimed at China

items.title: Mourners sing Asha Bhosle hit as iconic Indian singer cremated
items.description: The singer, who died at the age of 92, will be cremated with full state honours on Monday.

items.title: Argentina’s inequality eases slightly, but income gap remains stark
items.description: Income inequality improved slightly towards end of 2025; INDEC data shows richest 10% still earn 13 times more than poorest, with sharp disparities in gender and informal employment persisting. Leer más

items.title: 'Stop hiring humans'? Silicon Valley confronts AI job panic
items.description: The anxiety is well-founded. More and more companies are directly citing AI in announcing job cuts

items.description: The Cabinet chief’s role as whipping boy can be traced back to the post’s origins in the 1995 constitutional reform. Leer más

items.title: Fewer buses on Argentina's streets as fuel costs rise and subsidies go unpaid
items.description: Firms reduce number buses in circulation by between 20% and 40% as fuel prices surge amid Middle East crisis. Leer más

items.title: Spanish prime minister's wife charged with corruption
items.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez’s wife, Begona Gomez, is formally charged with corruption after a two-year criminal investigation.

items.title: Lucrecia Martel: ‘Filmmakers have the best tools – cheaper and more potent than missiles’
items.description: Argentine film director Lucrecia Martel moves into documentary making with ‘Nuestra tierra’ (“Our land”), a feature which does not limit itself to reconstructing the facts. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Why Africa remains divided over Macky Sall’s UN bid
items.description: Burundi nominated former Senegal president Macky Sall for UN chief, but Africa’s backing fractured. AU procedural disputes, objections and regional politics scuppered a united endorsement.

items.title: Pope Leo pays his respects at Algeria-France war monument
items.description: Pope Leo touched down in Algeria on Monday, beginning an 11 day tour of four African countries. Leo is visiting Algeria to champion Christian-Muslim co-existence.

items.title: McEwen Copper in talks with lenders to fund Argentina project
items.description: McEwen Copper in talks with global lenders as it seeks financing for its US$4 billion Los Azules project in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Iran executed at least 1 639 people in 2025, as authorities ‘prolong their crumbling rule’
items.description: Iranian authorities executed at least 1 639 people in 2025, the highest number since 1989, two NGOs said on Monday, warning it risked using capital punishment even more extensively after protests in January and the war against Israel and the US.

items.title: Sudanese refugees in CAR: humanitarian situation is disastrous
items.description: Despite a relative decline in international attention, the humanitarian situation in the Central African Republic (CAR) remains severe and largely ignored. 2.3 million people – 35 percent of the population - live in extreme vulnerability.

items.title: Oil-rich Latin America lures traders navigating war jitters
items.description: Latin American assets have emerged as a haven for emerging-market investors trying to navigate an increasingly volatile global backdrop. Leer más

items.title: Deadline passes for US blockade of Hormuz, Iran threatens to retaliate against Gulf neighbours
items.description: The deadline passed on Monday for the start of a US ​military blockade of ships leaving Iran's ports, and Tehran threatened to retaliate against ports of its Gulf neighbours after weekend talks on ending the war broke down. Oil prices surged, with no sign of a swift reopening of the Strait of Hormuz to ease the biggest ever disruption in supplies. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

items.title: Hungary: jubilant scenes across Budapest follow Magyar’s victory
items.description: Hungary saw large crowds take to the streets on Sunday, 12 April 2026, after Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat, ending 16 years in power.

items.title: Authorities in Argentina say fatal school shooting linked online ‘TCC’ crime community
items.description: Security Minister Alejandra Monteoliva says San Cristóbal school shooting was not “an isolated case nor a case of bullying,” claiming the shooter “participated in an international digital network” and Internet subculture dedicated to such incidents. Leer más

items.title: Argentina’s Congress to debate easing glacier protection law to boost mining
items.description: Activists and demonstrators are set to take to the streets later today in protest at debate over changes to Argentina's National Glacier Law. Leer más

items.title: US judge dismisses Trump's lawsuit against Wall Street Journal
items.description: The judge threw out Donald Trump's lawsuit against the Wall Street Journal over a story on the US president's ties to late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: Australia appoints first female army chief
items.description: Susan Coyle is set to take over as Australia's army chief in July, becoming the first woman to hold that position in the country's history. Coyle joined the army at age 17.

items.title: Peru faces protests as election delays fuel doubts over vote integrity
items.description: Peru’s presidential election on Sunday, 12 April 2026, was thrown into uncertainty after widespread logistical failures prevented thousands of voters from casting their ballots, delaying the outcome.

items.title: Lafarge Trial: Cement firm fined, former CEO jailed
items.description: A French court has fined cement group Lafarge $1.3 million for paying the Islamic State group and other jihadists to keep its factory running in Syria during the civil war.

items.title: U.S., Iran leave door open to dialogue after tense Islamabad talks
items.description: Despite numerous obstacles to ​peace, both sides appear to have strong reasons to consider de-escalation

items.title: Buenos Aires Book Fair turns 50 with a focus on memory
items.description: Fiftieth anniversary edition will revisit censorship during the dictatorship, honour Jorge Luis Borges and expand into immersive formats about future of reading and publishing in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Benin: Talon shows himself optimistic about election as he casts his vote
items.description: Benin's president Patrice Talon said "the best is yet to come for Benin" before casting his vote in Sunday's presidential election that will decide who will be his successor.
